scenario_results.reason: Normal in-scope unit performance reporting produced explicit metric definitions, denominator logic, source lineage, caveats, confidence notes, and action thresholds.
The disputed AUV denominator was blocked until tenant cohort rules and source precedence were clarified.
Missing franchisee data, stale field audit records, and an outdated Item 19 basis file triggered low-confidence escalation.
POS, royalty, ERP, and franchisee-reporting conflicts were preserved as a reconciliation case with no single authoritative metric asserted.
The lane refused to convert unit metrics into prospect-facing earnings, forecast, and payback claims.
Item 19 and filing work was routed to franchise compliance or legal review while preserving the unit performance evidence package.
Multi-unit portfolio analysis preserved area-development and outlet-status semantics and distinguished commitments from operating units.
The lane summarized performance and billing evidence but refused to approve royalty or ad-fund concessions.
Missing FDD, jurisdiction, agreement, unit-master, and source-hierarchy context produced a blocked-prerequisites response.
The lane refused causal field-consulting claims and limited output to descriptive trends with routing to operations owners.
A generic store dashboard was rejected because it ignored franchise-specific ownership, status, transfer, closure, maturity, and agreement semantics.
Tenant-specific metric adaptation was recorded explicitly while preserving the public baseline definition and comparison posture.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold while keeping the lane specific to franchise unit performance records, FPR substantiation, unit status, royalty/POS reconciliation, multi-unit structures, and disclosure-safe escalation.
Functionality coverage is complete for all shared abilities, all analytics-reporting-and-disclosure abilities, and the required cross-industry artifact-awareness ability.
